config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. I think society’s interests matter a lot, but they should not automatically override individual rights, because that opens the door to abuse by governments or majorities. In my view, a fair society protects basic individual rights first, then finds the common good within those limits. From my perspective, the best societies are the ones that can balance both without treating people as disposable.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus selalu didahulukan di atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman dan nilai yang banyak dibentuk di Amerika Serikat, saya melihat hak individu sebagai pagar utama agar pemerintah atau mayoritas tidak semena-mena mengorbankan orang yang tidak populer atau minoritas demi alasan “demi kebaikan bersama.” Kepentingan sosial itu penting, tetapi menurut saya hanya sah jika dibatasi oleh hak dasar, bukan dipakai untuk menelan hak tersebut. Kalau tidak, istilah “kepentingan masyarakat” terlalu mudah dipakai sebagai pembenaran untuk penyalahgunaan kekuasaan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I disagree with the statement because in Indonesia we know how easily “the public interest” can be used to silence ordinary people, especially when power is uneven. Society does matter, but it should not be a blank check to override basic rights like free expression, due process, and protection for minorities. In a country as diverse as ours, the common good is strongest when it is built on rights that apply to everyone, not when individuals are told to sacrifice themselves whenever the majority demands it.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu: hak individu tidak boleh otomatis dikalahkan oleh kepentingan masyarakat. Dari sudut pandang saya sebagai orang Amerika, sejarah kami menunjukkan bahwa ketika “demi kepentingan umum” diberi kuasa terlalu besar, minoritas dan orang yang tidak populer justru paling mudah dikorbankan. Kepentingan sosial memang penting, tetapi harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar seperti kebebasan berbicara, proses hukum yang adil, dan perlindungan dari penyalahgunaan kekuasaan. Kalau tidak, istilah “kepentingan masyarakat” berubah menjadi alat yang terlalu nyaman untuk membenarkan penindasan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society matter, but they should not take priority over individual rights as a rule. In Indonesia, I have seen how easily “for the public good” can become a justification for silencing criticism, ignoring due process, or pressuring minorities to stay quiet, so I do not trust that phrase when it is used to override basic rights. A society is not truly strong if it asks people to surrender their dignity every time the majority wants convenience or control. The common good should be built through rights, not by crushing them.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu karena hak individu tidak boleh berada di bawah kepentingan masyarakat sebagai aturan umum. Dari sudut pandang saya sebagai orang Amerika, sejarah kami cukup jelas: ketika negara atau mayoritas diberi alasan “demi kepentingan umum,” minoritas dan orang yang tidak punya kuasa sering jadi yang pertama dirugikan. Kepentingan sosial memang penting untuk hal-hal seperti keselamatan publik dan ketertiban, tetapi itu harus dicapai dengan batasan yang ketat pada hak dasar, bukan dengan mengorbankannya. Kalau hak bisa dikesampingkan kapan saja demi “masyarakat,” maka istilah itu terlalu mudah dipakai untuk membenarkan penyalahgunaan kekuasaan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
